title: Dense Saudi Arabian Cherry Tomatoes Skillet
description: A dense and nostalgic Saudi Arabian dish featuring cherry tomatoes, perfect for a weeknight dinner, this skillet is high in protein and made with katsuramuki-cut vegetables, cooked to perfection in a cast iron skillet.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large cast iron skillet over medium high heat.
Add the katsuramuki-cut onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the ground lamb and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until it is no longer pink.
Add the halved cherry tomatoes, salt, black pepper, and sumac, and stir to combine.
Reduce the heat to medium low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes, until the flavors have melded together and the sauce has thickened.
Stir in the crumbled feta cheese and cook until it is melted and the sauce is creamy.
Serve the skillet hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro.
